text: Ever wondered why islands are biodiversity hotspots? Island biogeography studies how species thrive or struggle in isolated ecosystems. More species? Bigger islands! But, why is that? Research shows larger islands support greater diversity due to resources and habitat variety. Yet, isolation can lead to unique adaptations—think giant tortoises on the Galapagos! Curious about more science mysteries? Like and follow for more!
